target.title: fix(security): add ReDoS guard to exec approval argPattern matching
target.body:
## Problem

`matchArgPattern` in `src/infra/exec-command-resolution.ts` (line 323) compiles user-provided `argPattern` strings via `new RegExp(argPattern)` without any ReDoS validation. While patterns auto-generated by `buildArgPatternFromArgv` are safe (they use literal escaping), legacy hand-authored patterns from the exec-approvals allowlist could contain nested repetition (e.g., `(a+)+$`) that causes catastrophic backtracking.

The codebase already has a safe-regex module at `src/security/safe-regex.ts` with `compileSafeRegex()` that detects nested repetition, but it was not wired into this call site.

## Fix

Replace `new RegExp(argPattern)` with `compileSafeRegex(argPattern)`. If the pattern fails validation (nested repetition or invalid regex), return `false` (no match) instead of compiling the dangerous pattern.

## Real behavior proof

### Red-green verification

Tests were red-green verified by temporarily reverting the guard (`compileSafeRegex` back to `new RegExp`) and confirming the new tests fail, then restoring the guard and confirming all pass.

**GREEN (with guard):** 25 passed, 0 failed
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts
 ✓ |infra| exec-command-resolution.test.ts (25 tests) 102ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  25 passed (25)
```

**RED (guard reverted to `new RegExp(argPattern)`):** 3 failed, 22 passed
```
 × rejects nested-repetition variants beyond the classic (a+)+$
     ([a-z]+)*$ was NOT rejected — matchAllowlist returned the entry instead of null
 × matchAllowlist rejects all known ReDoS vectors end-to-end
     ([a-z]+)*$ was NOT rejected — matchAllowlist returned the entry instead of null
 × returns within a bounded time even when given a ReDoS payload
     expected 11468 ms to be less than 500 ms — real catastrophic backtracking!
```

The timing test is the strongest proof: without the guard, `(a+)+$` against `"a".repeat(30) + "!"` took **11,468 ms** (catastrophic backtracking). With the guard, it completes in < 1 ms because the pattern is rejected at compile time.

### Test coverage summary (10 tests in `matchArgPattern ReDoS safety via compileSafeRegex`)

| Test | What it proves |
|------|---------------|
| `rejects ReDoS-vulnerable argPattern instead of hanging` | Classic `(a+)+$` via matchAllowlist returns null |
| `still matches a safe regex argPattern correctly` | `^script\.py$` matches/rejects correctly |
| `rejects an invalid regex argPattern gracefully` | `[unclosed` returns null, no throw |
| `rejects nested-repetition variants` | `(\d+)+$`, `([a-z]+)*$`, `(x+x+)+y` all rejected |
| `falls back to path-only entry when argPattern is ReDoS-unsafe` | Path-only entry returned when argPattern entry is rejected |
| `compileSafeRegex directly rejects ReDoS patterns that new RegExp would accept` | **Before/after proof**: `new RegExp(pattern)` succeeds (vulnerability), `compileSafeRegex(pattern)` returns null (guard) for 7 attack vectors |
| `compileSafeRegexDetailed returns unsafe-nested-repetition reason` | Structured reason is `"unsafe-nested-repetition"`, not generic error |
| `compileSafeRegex accepts safe patterns and returns working RegExp instances` | 5 safe patterns compile and match correctly |
| `matchAllowlist rejects all known ReDoS vectors end-to-end` | 8 attack vectors through full integration path all return null |
| `returns within a bounded time even when given a ReDoS payload` | `"a".repeat(30) + "!"` completes < 500 ms (guard rejects at compile time) |

### Attack vectors tested
`(a+)+$`, `(\d+)+$`, `([a-z]+)*$`, `(x+x+)+y`, `(a|a?)+$`, `(.*a){25}`, `(\w+)+$`, `(a|aa)+$`

### What was not tested
Live exec approval with a ReDoS-vulnerable argPattern in production config.
target.author: sebtardif
